mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing present values of future corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate hike periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to produce a positive return probability above 55% over the 60-day horizon in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during post-shock recovery periods or produces negative returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above the threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in > 58% of observations over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending capacity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to achieve a positive return frequency above 55% or shows negative rank correlation over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases or fail to maintain a positive information coefficient over 3-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces corporate profit forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks fail to predict negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
